code_change.description: Add diagnostic --batch-sample-argmax-audit / HIPENGINE_QWEN35_BATCH_SAMPLE_ARGMAX_AUDIT. It runs batch_argmax_f32, copies the batch output used for generation, then runs serial per-row argmax over the same batched logits and accumulates mismatch stats. Audit records a sampler blocker and does not make retained throughput claims.

conclusion: Five post-c9 grouped-stress c2 runs with explicit batched_lm_head + batch_argmax_f32 plus same-logits serial argmax audit stayed generated-token green ([137,137]) and checked 680 decode steps / 1360 row argmaxes with zero batch-vs-serial token mismatches. This does not reproduce the prior [103,137] flake, and it rules out a deterministic batch_argmax_f32 reduction disagreement on these audited logits. The audit is diagnostic-only, records a sampler blocker, and makes no throughput claim; c2 default remains serial_lm_head until the explicit batched sampler flake is either reproduced under audit or otherwise fixed.
remaining_blockers: explicit c2 batched_lm_head still has historical post-c9 flake evidence from iter222 and remains blocked as a default/retained sampler path
audit mode can now catch same-logits batch-vs-serial argmax disagreement if the flake reproduces
full-native/no-rowchunk grouped full-attention remains the retained c4/c8 blocker

batch_blockers: native c>N decode currently supports compact physical-slot-ordered rows; full-attention batch context is native only for BF16 KV and context < 1024
native c>N decode is experimental and blocked until generated-token equality passes
linear-attention projections forced to selected-c1 diagnostic path
full-attention decode forced to native row-chunk diagnostic path
full-attention O projection forced to batch GEMV for nonzero row chunks
projection dispatch: no c-aware projection candidate applies to this row count
decode_blockers: linear-attention projections forced to selected-c1 diagnostic path
full-attention decode forced to native row-chunk diagnostic path
full-attention O projection forced to batch GEMV for nonzero row chunks
runs.label: c9-grouped-rowchunk2-before-c2-batched-argmax-audit

workload.cycle_command_shape: for each cycle: run known-red c9 grouped rowchunk2, then immediately run c2 batched LM-head with batch_argmax_f32 plus same-logits serial argmax audit in a new Python process
